key_insight: CORAL trains a reinforcement-learning docking model to capture the cooperative, groove-stacking binding geometry unique to amyloid fibrils, rather than treating them like globular protein targets.
engineering_relevance: The method could improve structure-guided discovery of amyloid-binding diagnostics and therapeutics by producing more realistic ligand poses and better affinity ranking for fibril targets.
method: Uses reinforcement learning to generate ligand pose distributions for amyloid fibrils.
Adds a reward that combines protein-ligand docking affinity with cooperative ligand-ligand stacking energy.
Targets the cross-β groove geometry characteristic of amyloid fibrils.
evidence_signal: The paper reports evaluation on experimentally resolved structures plus a curated amyloid-ligand set built from model-generated poses validated by domain experts, with improved pose quality and affinity correlation over baselines.
watch_item: Performance may depend on the quality of the cooperative-energy reward and the limited, curated evaluation data, so broader validation on more real amyloid complexes is still needed.
paper_title: CORAL: Learning Amyloid Fibril Ligand Docking with Cooperative Binding Rewards
